TextENGNew: ``Ah... yeah, I remember you mentioning something like that.``
``Ha ha ha.@k@vS12/08/280800003. But why?@k@vS12/08/280800004. Why would anyone want to murder Takano-san and me?``
``My, my.@k@vS12/09/280900002. Being named as victims of the fifth curse? I'd consider that an honor!@k@vS12/09/280900003. Hee hee.``
...Just as I expected, neither of them took my warning about being killed tonight seriously.
They did keep their promise to strengthen my security in exchange for showing them the ritual storehouse, at least...
But when it came to their own safety, they hadn't given it a single thought.
Well... I hadn't expected them to believe me anyway.
If they were willing to take me at my word, I could have changed their fate long ago.

``...I'll say this one more time.@k@vS12/05/280500023. ...Tomitake, you're going to claw your own throat out.@k@vS12/05/280500024. Takano, you're going to be burned to death somewhere deep in the mountains.``
``Claw my own throat out... are you talking about the terminal symptoms of L5?``
``Don't worry. I've been receiving my injections on schedule.@k@vS12/08/280800007. There's no way that could happen to me.``
``My fate isn't exactly pleasant either.@k@vS12/09/280900005. Burned alive on some remote mountainside? How rude.@k@vS12/09/280900006. ...Did you see all this in a dream?``
``.........I just need the two of you to be careful, at least for tonight.@k@vS12/05/280500026. Please listen to what I'm telling you, even if you think it's nonsense.``

``You had some kind of prophetic dream about danger befalling us?@k@vS12/09/280900008. Don't worry.@k@vS12/09/280900009. The Mountain Dogs are guarding you around the clock.@k@vS12/09/280900010. Even today, eight operatives have been watching over you.``
``...Thank you.@k@vS12/05/280500028. I'm grateful for the security around me.@k@vS12/05/280500029. So please, I'm asking you to be just as careful yourselves.``
``Thank you for worrying about us.@k@vS12/08/280800009. We'll be fine. I may not look it, but I stay in shape.@k@vS12/08/280800010. If anyone comes after us, I can handle it.``
Tomitake struck a pose, flexing his muscles, and flashed a smile.
He was probably trying to put me at ease.@k But that groundless smile only made me feel sadder.
...This is the tragedy of being a bystander.
I know the future, yet I have no way to make them believe me.
It's as though the world were designed to prevent interference.@k People simply don't believe prophecies.
``...But you're going to die.@k@vS12/05/280500031. If Tomitake and Takano die, I'll be next.@k@vS12/05/280500032. Once you're both dead, who will protect me?``
``It sounds like you had a truly terrible dream.@k@vS12/09/280900012. ...Don't worry.@k@vS12/09/280900013. Even if something were to happen to us, the Mountain Dogs would still protect you.``
``...Are you sure they really would?@k@vS12/05/280500034. They've never actually protected me before.``
``That's not true.@k@vS12/09/280900015. They came when you got into that fight, didn't they?@k@vS12/09/280900016. They're keeping a constant watch over you.``

``Of course, under normal circumstances the security isn't quite that tight. But ever since your recent request, the alert level around you has been maintained at an extremely high standard.@k@vS12/09/280900018. Even more thorough than Director Irie's own security detail.``
``...Will that level of security be maintained even after Tomitake and Takano are dead?``
``Of course.@k@vS12/09/280900020. Nothing matters more than your safety.``
``Ahahahaha...@k@vS12/08/280800012. So you've already decided we're getting killed tonight?@k@vS12/08/280800013. Well, that's a bit much.``
``...If Tomitake has no will to survive, then all I care about is what happens to me after you're gone.``
My cold words seemed to finally reach Tomitake's heart.
His expression became just a little more serious.
``...Good grief.@k@vS12/08/280800015. All right.@k@vS12/08/280800016. I'll be extra careful tonight.@k@vS12/08/280800017. When I head back to Okinomiya, I'll bring a security escort.``
``I believe you'll be attacked when the two of you are alone together.@k@vS12/05/280500038. So whatever you do, don't be alone with each other tonight.``
``Hee hee hee...@k@vS12/09/280900022. Oh my, could you be jealous?@k@vS12/09/280900023. What a precocious little girl.``
...As I feared, there was no changing their fate.
When we saved Satoko, everyone shared the same sense of crisis.
That was why we united so quickly and pulled off the miracle of rescuing her before catastrophe struck.
But these two had no sense of danger whatsoever.
How could you make people without any sense of urgency take something seriously?
I let out a sigh of mild resignation.
``...Tomitake, Takano.@k@vS12/05/280500040. Goodbye.@k@vS12/05/280500041. Thank you for everything.``
``Wait a moment... Rika-chan...!``
There was no point saying anything more to them.
Instead, I would try warning someone else.
If I could get others to be on guard as well, even if these two refused, perhaps it might throw fate off course just enough.
Don't give up because it seems hopeless.@k Think of fate as something as thin and fragile as a goldfish-scooping net.
Like the lid on a jar of jam.
It won't open unless you twist it believing it will.
I have to believe that fate can be changed.@k Otherwise, it never will be.
``Tomitake-san and Takano-san, you say?@k@vS12/10/281000002. ...Is this really true?``
Naturally, I'd told Irie about this many times before, since he was the person closest to them both.
Compared to Tomitake and Takano, Irie at least listened with relative seriousness.@k ...But whether he could actually do anything about it was another matter entirely.
``...Is a bad premonition from Oyashiro-sama's shrine maiden not reason enough for you?``
``Ha ha ha ha.@k@vS12/10/281000004. ...I understand.``

``If you put it that way, it might very well be a divine message from Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS12/10/281000006. If something like that happened tonight, it would make five consecutive years.@k@vS12/10/281000007. I'll warn both of them to be on their guard.``
The most Irie could do was urge the two of them to be careful.
Which was essentially the same thing I'd already done myself.@k ...In other words, it wouldn't make much difference.
Irie was a researcher, not a crisis manager equipped to handle emergencies.@k ...That role was meant to be Takano's.
``...I implore you, please.@k@vS12/05/280500044. *bow*``
Who would take me seriously?
Who could actually prevent Tomitake and Takano's deaths?
That's right -- there was one man who burned with passion to fight Oyashiro-sama's curse.
``Oh, Furude-san.@k@vS12/11/280700005. I've been looking for you.@k@vS12/11/280700006. Akasaka-san wanted to say a proper goodbye before he left.``
``...When it truly matters, Akasaka and I are never in the same place.``
``Na ha ha!@k@vS12/11/280700008. You're still upset about that hot spring incident, aren't you?@k@vS12/11/280700009. Akasaka-san really regretted that he wasn't able to repay your kindness.``
``He said he'd love to come back to Hinamizawa when he gets a longer vacation.@k@vS12/11/280700011. Na ha ha!@k@vS12/11/280700012. Apparently his wife got the wrong idea about that.``
``She's cute, his wife, but I bet she can be scary at home.@k@vS12/11/280700014. Nfu fu fu!``
``...Ooishi.@k@vS12/05/280500047. ...There's something important I need to tell you, but no one will believe me.``
I'd approached Ooishi with this before, several times.
changeReason: Adds character name 'Ooishi' not present in original translation.

TextENGNew: He usually listened with reasonable seriousness.
But after the murders, he'd start to suspect me. That was why I'd been reluctant to go to him lately.
``Where did you hear that?@k@vS12/11/280700018. Where in the festival crowd?``
Rather than telling him it was a dream, I said I'd overheard someone plotting in the crowd.
...That way, he might not develop strange suspicions about me after the murders actually happened.

``...It's true that Tomitake-san is an outsider, and with Takano-san, that makes two.@k@vS12/11/280700020. One dies and one disappears...@k@vS12/11/280700021. ...It's certainly plausible as a fifth year of the curse...``
``It could just be a false rumor, but understood.@k@vS12/11/280700023. Nothing ventured, nothing gained.@k@vS12/11/280700024. I'll have someone keep an eye on the two of them.``
``I think the people I overheard were saying they would attack somewhere on the road between Hinamizawa and Okinomiya.``
Tomitake's body would be found on the road between the village and Okinomiya.
...If I recalled correctly, it was always discovered near where the paved road turned to gravel.
``Understood.@k@vS12/11/280700026. I'll station a patrol car along that road.@k@vS12/11/280700027. We can manage that much, at least.``
Ooishi agreed to help without questioning my story.
changeReason: Adds character name 'Ooishi' not present in original translation.

TextENGNew: Most people would be stunned that someone like Ooishi would take such a dubious tip at face value.
Apparently, his guiding principle was 'where there's smoke, there's fire.' He had a habit of taking tip-offs like these seriously.@k I'd learned that across countless worlds.
changeReason: Removes character name 'Ooishi' from original translation.

TextENGNew: ...Of course, that same endearing quirk had also complicated things terribly in some worlds...
But it wasn't hard to understand why.
He was desperate to expose Oyashiro-sama's curse, and this year, with his retirement looming, was his last chance.
Even if it might be a false lead, he couldn't bring himself to ignore it.
That worked in my favor, since I had no proof to offer.
``...Hey, Kuma-chan, you read me? Can you hear me?@k@vS12/11/280700029. Station a patrol car along the road back to Okinomiya.``

``...No, no, just a precaution.@k@vS12/11/280700031. Got a tip that something bad might go down around there tonight.@k@vS12/11/280700032. Yeah, two officers should be enough.@k@vS12/11/280700033. Thank you.``
``...Thank you.@k@vS12/05/280500050. ...Nobody else would believe me, and I didn't know what to do.``
``It's my job to take these things seriously.@k@vS12/11/280700035. If nothing happens, we should be happy about that.``

``Rejoice in a false alarm! --@k@vS12/11/280700037. Words of a senior colleague of mine.@k@vS12/11/280700038. Besides, ignoring a tip and failing to prevent a crime... there's nothing worse than that kind of regret.``
...I see.@k Ooishi had his own philosophy behind his willingness to believe.
changeReason: Adds character name 'Ooishi' not present in original translation.

TextENGNew: ...For the moment, at least, that philosophy made him the most reliable ally I could ask for.
If only, after tomorrow, once Tomitake and Takano turned up dead, he wouldn't treat me as a suspect...
...I shouldn't worry about that now.
There was still more to do.@k Who else could I talk to?
...Should I try telling my friends...?
Don't assume it's pointless. At least try...
``No way!@k@vS12/03/280300027. Someone was actually saying something that alarming?``
``On the night of the festival, of all nights? That's really tasteless.``
``You mean the curse, right?@k@vS12/01/280100022. The curse of Oyashiro-sama that might happen tonight?``
``Yeah.@k@vS12/03/280300030. It's happened four years running now.@k@vS12/03/280300031. Seems like quite a few people think it'll happen again tonight.``
``People can believe whatever they want, but naming Tomitake-san and Takano-san specifically is rather rude.``
``I think you should probably warn them, just in case, I think.``
``...I already did, but they didn't believe me.``
``Can't say I'm surprised.@k@vS12/01/280100024. ...But if something actually happens, it'll be too late.@k@vS12/01/280100025. You should talk to Ooishi-san.``
``...I already have.``
``Oh? You've been quite thorough, Rika-chama.``
``But you're right, better safe than sorry.@k@vS12/06/280600012. ...Especially tonight.``

``The serial mysterious deaths of Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/03/280300033. Commonly known as the curse of Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS12/03/280300034. ...Honestly, having this keep going year after year is a real problem.@k@vS12/03/280300035. Copycats start coming out of the woodwork, and it's terrible for the village's reputation.``
``In other words, if nothing happens tonight, the streak is broken, right?@k@vS12/01/280100027. Then we should do whatever we can to make sure nothing happens.``
``What exactly can we do, though?@k@vS12/04/280400016. Patrol the entire village to prevent something bad from happening?``
``Patrolling would probably be counterproductive.@k@vS12/06/280600014. Is there really anything we can do?``
``The Watanagashi preparation committee has been warning people to watch for unfamiliar faces, in case someone tries to imitate the serial killings.``
``In Hinamizawa, everyone knows everyone.@k@vS12/02/280200019. We'd spot a stranger immediately.@k@vS12/02/280200020. So I think we'll be fine, probably...``
``...But even so, Tomitake and Takano will still be killed.``
``Rika-chan...@k@vS12/02/280200022. ...You're right. Let's try to do whatever we can.``

``Agreed!@k@vS12/01/280100029. We can't guard them all night, but at least until the festival ends, let's stay close to them.@k@vS12/01/280100030. Nobody's going to try anything while we're right there.``
I couldn't tell whether my friends truly understood, but they were at least trying to take my words seriously.
We agreed to find Tomitake and Takano and keep watch over them.
Had I done everything within my power?
...But it was hard to believe this would be enough to prevent their deaths...
``...All that's left is to see whether anything has happened when I wake up tomorrow morning.``
``...Do you really think you can prevent Tomitake and Takano's deaths, Rika?``
``...It might be impossible.@k@vS12/05/280500056. Perhaps their deaths are the tolling bell that signals the end of my time.``

``But Takano said it herself earlier.@k@vS12/05/280500058. Even if those two die, the Mountain Dogs are supposed to continue protecting me.@k@vS12/05/280500059. Reinforcements from Tokyo might arrive in the meantime, too.``
``...Even if Tomitake and Takano die as they always do, what follows will be different from every other world.``
``............What if it still isn't enough?``

``...Then I'll have no choice but to die with grace.@k@vS12/05/280500062. ...But this time, I won't give up until the very end.@k@vS12/05/280500063. I'll struggle as long as I have strength to struggle.@k@vS12/05/280500064. I refuse to surrender and spend my final hours drinking wine, telling myself it's all pointless.``
``.........Do whatever feels right to you, Rika.``
``...Do you still believe it's all hopeless, Hanyuu?``
``.........It's not about hopeless or hopeful. What will be, will be. That is simply fate.``
``Hoping is what makes the pain worse, is that it?``
If I was an actress trying to take the stage, perhaps Hanyuu was a bystander in the truest sense.
At times like these, the rift between us grew wide...
``...Rika.@k@vS12/12/281100010. Whatever happens, please... don't lose heart...``
``That sounds like a roundabout way of telling me this will fail too.@k@vS12/05/280500068. ...Lately, talking to you has been getting on my nerves.``
I understood, really.
Hanyuu told me not to get my hopes up because she cared about me.
Because I dared to hope I could defeat fate, the betrayal of that hope left me wounded.
If I kept getting wounded, I would eventually break down and die.
For Hanyuu, that would mean losing the only companion she'd ever found.
Nothing could be more unbearable for Hanyuu, who lived in solitude, unable to communicate with anyone else.
...I believed that fate could be broken.
Even when I lacked that conviction, I still believed that my destiny was like a maze -- that after hundreds of wrong turns, there had to be an exit somewhere.
But in truth... perhaps Hanyuu already knew.
That we were goldfish, and our fate was the fishbowl.@k That there had never been an exit to begin with.
Inside that fishbowl, with only each other for all eternity, I threw myself against the glass wall day after day, believing I could one day shatter it.
...And Hanyuu, telling me it was futile.
If I kept getting hurt and died, the loneliness awaiting her would be unimaginable. Of course she wanted to change my mind.
...When I thought of it this way, the gap between Hanyuu's and my understanding of fate made perfect sense.
Hanyuu had lived far, far longer than I had.
...Because of that, she understood the laws of this infinitely expanding world far more deeply than I ever could.
...No matter how fiercely I defied Hanyuu... I might never be able to defy the truth itself.
Even so... I had to hold fast to what I believed.
The weapon that shatters fate is a spear called conviction. The strength of one's belief is what hones its edge.
Together with my friends, we found Tomitake and stayed close, chatting to keep him nearby as long as possible.
But when the festival wound down, the two of them tried to leave.
If they slipped away now, I would never see them again.
Mion suggested they stay the night at the main Sonozaki house, but Tomitake firmly declined.
At first, everyone tried to keep them from going off alone, warning that someone might be plotting against them.

But eventually, imaginations turned to more... romantic reasons why the two might want to be alone,@k and before I knew it, people were telling me not to interfere with two lovers on their last night together.
``Don't worry, Rika-chan.@k@vS12/02/280200024. Tomitake-san is a man, after all.@k@vS12/02/280200025. When push comes to shove, he'll fight tooth and nail to protect Takano-san.``
``Is that so?@k@vS12/09/280900025. If I'm ever in danger, you'll put your life on the line to save me, won't you, Jirou-san?``
``Ah, ahahahaha... Th-that's the plan!``
``Well, we really should get going.@k@vS12/02/280200027. It's getting awfully late.``
``Thank you for being so thoughtful.@k@vS12/09/280900027. Hee hee...``
``P-please don't get the wrong idea!!@k@vS12/08/280800021. I left something at the clinic.@k@vS12/08/280800022. Takano-san has the key, so we have to go together!``
``Oh my, the clinic? How romantic, Tomitake-san. Playing doctor, are we?``
``That's not it, Shion~. He's obviously going to have her put on a nurse outfit for a photo shoot.@k@vS12/03/280300038. Heh heh heh!``
Crash, bang, wham!!
``Hauuu!!@k@vS12/02/280200029. I wonder what kind of photos they'll take in nurse outfits, I wonder!``

``If you understand perfectly well, stop hitting people to hide your embarrassment...@k@vS12/01/280100032. And you two --@k@vS12/01/280100033. stop only getting along when you're making dirty jokes...``

``...W-well then, goodbye everyone...@k@vS12/08/280800024. Ahahaha...@k@vS12/08/280800025. I really am just going to pick up something I forgot...@k@vS12/08/280800026. Don't follow us, okay...?@k@vS12/08/280800027. Ahahahaha...``
``Ahahahahahahahahahahahahaha... Hauuu!``
``...Tomitake... please, truly, be careful...``
``I think you're worrying too much, Rika.@k@vS12/04/280400018. ...Hwaaaah...``
Everyone laughed when Satoko let out a big yawn.
It seemed like the right signal to call it a night.
...At first glance, it didn't seem like I'd changed their fate at all.
But if all this commotion left them even slightly more alert than usual as they faced their fated deaths,@k ...perhaps some small shift could alter their destiny.
All that remained was to pray.
``Goodbye, Tomitake-san!@k@vS12/03/280300040. Make sure you show us those photos next time!``
``And don't forget to wear that punishment game shirt next time!``
``Oh man, a night with a beautiful woman -- I'm so jealous! Woo-hoo!``
``Ah, ahahahaha.@k@vS12/08/280800029. See you all again.@k@vS12/08/280800030. Next time will probably be around fall.``
``Assuming we don't get killed tonight, that is.@k@vS12/09/280900029. Hee hee.@k@vS12/09/280900030. Good night, everyone.``
````Good night~!````
That was the best I could do on the night of Watanagashi.
